title: lithocarpus pakhaensis
intro: Lithocarpus pakhaensis, also known as Pakha oak, is a species of tree in the beech family, Fagaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, fever, and inflammation, due to its medicinal properties. The health benefits of Lithocarpus pakhaensis include its ability to reduce oxidative stress, improve liver function, and exhibit antimicrobial activity. In traditional herbal preparations, the plant is often made into tea, infused in oil, or used as a tincture to leverage its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
